label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking what the dance project aims to do. The options are A to D. First, I need to read the passage again to understand the main purpose of the project. The passage talks about LaMar Baylor teaching dance to street children in Rwanda. The Rebecca Davis Dance Company's project helps them learn dance and how to behave in a classroom. Rebecca Davis thought dance could get them off the street and into a safe place, leading to education. She mentions that after learning to attend classes and follow directions, they can take IT classes. Also, some boys get scholarships to a boarding school. Looking at the options: A is parental care. The passage says the children have lost their families, but the project doesn't mention providing parental figures, just dance and education. So probably not A. B is scholarships. While scholarships are mentioned, they are a result or part of the program for those who do best, not the main aim. The main aim seems broader than just scholarships. C is helping them receive education. The project starts with dance classes to get them into a structured environment, leading to possible IT classes and scholarships. So the main aim is education
